regions.eastern-caribbean:
Eastern Caribbean Weather Briefing for March 26, 2026:

Winds across the region are easterly at 5-14 knots with moderate seas. San Juan reports 74°F, partly cloudy skies, and a 40% chance of isolated showers this morning. Expect increasing thunderstorm activity today through March 28, with rain probabilities peaking at 88% today and lingering through the period. Mariners should monitor thunderstorm development carefully, especially near San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Maarten, where squalls and gusty winds may reduce visibility and cause rapidly changing conditions. Yacht and fishing captains should secure loose gear and avoid exposed anchorages during storms, while cruise passengers are advised to plan indoor activities during afternoon thunderstorms. No Small Craft Advisories or marine warnings are in effect at this time.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean weather is stable today with clear skies and light northeast winds 8 knots at Cozumel, temperature steady near 78°F. Expect winds to increase from the east-northeast 13-15 knots over the next three days with partly cloudy skies; rain chances rise to 23% on March 27 and 46% on March 28. Mariners should monitor conditions closely at Belize City and Roatan where afternoon showers could reduce visibility and affect small craft operations. Cruise and fishing captains are advised to plan for moderate seas and possible wet decks later in the week, securing gear and reviewing shelter options at Grand Cayman and Ocho Rios.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region weather for March 26, 2026: Current conditions in Nassau show 71°F with light northeast winds at 6 knots under overcast skies and no rain expected today. Through the next three days, expect steady east-northeast winds increasing from 12 to 18 knots, temperatures holding near 75-81°F, with mostly overcast skies and minimal rain chances, except light drizzle possible on day three. Mariners should note that winds remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds, but increasing sea state may impact smaller vessels, particularly near Freeport and Bimini anchorages. Cruise and fishing captains should plan for reduced visibility due to overcast conditions and occasional drizzle, and passengers should carry light rain gear while ashore.
regions.southern-caribbean:
Southern Caribbean Weather Briefing – March 26, 2026

Aruba currently reports 79°F with east winds steady at 15 knots under partly cloudy skies and minimal rain risk. Over the next three days, expect consistent easterly winds 16-17 knots and predominantly overcast conditions with rain chances under 10 percent. Ports at Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena will experience similar trade wind patterns, maintaining good visibility and stable marine conditions. Yacht and fishing captains should secure light to moderate sail setups; anticipate smooth anchorage in protected harbors like Cartagena’s Bahía de Cartagena. Cruise passengers can enjoy outdoor activities with low rain risk but should plan for occasional cloud cover. No marine warnings are in effect.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean daily briefing for March 26, 2026: Grand Cayman reports 78°F with ENE winds at 13 knots under overcast skies and a 20% chance of rain. Expect increasing ENE winds 16-18 knots over the next three days with frequent thunderstorms and rain chances rising to 35-66 percent. Roatan, Belize City, Cozumel, and Costa Maya will experience similar moderate trade winds and scattered storms, reducing visibility and causing choppy seas. Captains should secure loose gear and monitor radar closely; fishing operations may face intermittent interruptions. Passengers should be advised to carry rain gear and avoid exposed decks during thunderstorms, especially near Grand Cayman and Belize City anchorages.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles weather for March 26, 2026: Barbados reports 78°F with ENE winds at 16 knots under partly cloudy skies and no rain. Expect steady trade winds 13-17 knots with mostly overcast conditions and minimal rain chances (5-13%) through Day 3. Mariners should anticipate moderate easterly trades; small craft operators in exposed anchorages around St. Lucia and Martinique should secure vessels as wind speeds approach 17 knots. Cruise and fishing captains can plan nearshore operations with low risk of showers but maintain vigilance for localized squalls typical in overcast conditions.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles region weather for March 26, 2026: Current conditions at Los Angeles / San Pedro show 59°F with light northeast winds at 4 knots under overcast skies and zero precipitation chance. Expect a steady west wind 11 knots today with highs near 75°F and continued overcast conditions; seas should remain calm for all ports including Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island. Tomorrow’s forecast calls for lighter west-southwest winds at 9 knots with fog expected in the morning, reducing visibility—exercise caution entering or leaving harbors, especially Catalina’s West End anchorage. Day three warms to 84°F with west winds near 11 knots and overcast skies; no marine advisories are in effect, but keep a close watch for fog and adjust navigation plans accordingly.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada area conditions today feature light SSE winds at 3 knots and partly cloudy skies with no rain expected. Visibility may be reduced by fog through the next two days under light WSW winds near 7-8 knots, especially around Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos, so maintain vigilant navigation and use appropriate AIS and radar aids. Temperatures will rise from the low 70s to upper 80s by day three with overcast skies but minimal precipitation risk. Vessel operators should prepare for calm seas and stable conditions with no marine wind warnings; fishing captains can expect good conditions but remain alert for localized fog in the early mornings.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas region will see mild northwest winds steady around 10-12 knots with temperatures near 73-75°F through March 26-28. Skies will be partly cloudy today, becoming overcast the following two days, with no rain expected and calm seas. Mariners should expect stable conditions for navigation or fishing; moderate winds and clear visibility support safe operations at Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz anchorages. Cruise passengers can enjoy outdoor activities with minimal weather impact, but remain aware of overcast skies developing after today.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region is experiencing mild conditions today with light east winds at 1 knot, partly cloudy skies, and no rain expected. Through the next three days, winds will shift from south-southwest to west-northwest, increasing to 8-11 knots with mostly partly cloudy skies, though overcast conditions and a 23% rain chance are expected by day three. Mariners should note calm sea state at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata ports, with no Small Craft Advisories in effect. Fishing captains and yacht operators can plan for generally favorable conditions but remain alert for increasing cloud cover and the slight risk of light showers later in the week.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region conditions today feature light west winds at 2 knots and partly cloudy skies with temperatures near 78°F. Over the next three days, expect steady westerly winds increasing to 8-10 knots with mostly overcast to partly cloudy skies and minimal rain chances under 5 percent. Mariners should find calm to moderate conditions for navigation and anchoring in Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala harbors. Fishing captains should plan for stable seas but monitor cloud cover for any localized squalls; cruise passengers can enjoy outdoor activities with low risk of precipitation.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean briefing for March 26, 2026: Barcelona currently reports 51°F with light north winds near 1 knot and partly cloudy skies; rain chance remains minimal at 3%. Expect increasing southerly winds to 10 knots tomorrow with intermittent rain showers near 43% probability, shifting to overcast but drier conditions by day two. Fog may reduce visibility near coastal areas including Palma de Mallorca and Ibiza on day three, so exercise caution when maneuvering or anchoring. Yacht and fishing captains should monitor for reduced visibility in the early mornings and prepare for light to moderate southerly winds; cruise passengers should pack layers and rain gear for intermittent showers especially around Valencia and Malaga. No marine warnings are in effect at this time.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean briefing for March 26, 2026: Naples conditions currently stable with 57°F, WSW winds at 16 knots, partly cloudy skies, and no rain expected today. However, rain showers with a 95% probability and sustained WSW winds near 17 knots are forecast for tomorrow, which may reduce visibility and increase wave action, particularly impacting anchorage safety near Naples and Civitavecchia. By day three, winds shift to NNW at 6 knots with overcast skies and no rain, improving conditions for small craft operations around Sardinia, Corsica, and the northern Adriatic ports such as Split and Dubrovnik. Yacht and fishing captains should secure vessels ahead of tomorrow’s showers and WSW winds; cruise passengers advised to plan indoor activities during wet periods.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Eastern Mediterranean Forecast for March 26, 2026: Athens (Piraeus) currently reports clear skies, light southwest winds at 2 knots, and 58°F. Expect mild southerly winds increasing to 7 knots with overcast skies today and no rain. By tomorrow, southeasterly winds rise to 9 knots with a 55% chance of rain showers, transitioning to overcast and lighter westerly winds on day three. Mariners should maintain caution near Rhodes and Cyprus where afternoon showers may reduce visibility; small craft operators should monitor conditions closely but no advisories are in effect. Passengers should plan outdoor activities in Athens and Mykonos early today before cloud cover increases and possible rain arrives tomorrow.
